activities_projects.title: Socio-Economic Disparity Research
activities_projects.objective: Critically analyze the gap between political equality and economic reality
materials: Case studies of Kanta vs. Jain Saheb
Internet for data on income inequality
steps: Identify the differences in 'Living Conditions' and 'Access to Healthcare' for Kanta and the Jains
Research the current Gini Coefficient of India to understand income inequality
Write a report on how lack of money limits the actual 'Equality' promised by the Universal Adult Franchise
Argue whether political equality can truly exist without economic equality
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates the structural limitations of democracy in achieving absolute equality
activities_projects.evaluation: Critical thinking depth and evidence-based arguments (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Constitutional Law & Landmark Judgments
activities_projects.objective: Examine the legal enforcement of Article 15
materials: Briefs of landmark cases on discrimination
steps: Deconstruct Article 15(1) and 15(2) regarding access to shops, restaurants, and wells
Analyze why 'Untouchability' (Article 17) required a separate constitutional abolition despite Article 15
Draft a 'Legal Petition' for a hypothetical case where a person is denied entry to a park based on their caste
Explain the role of the Judiciary as the protector of these rights
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Synthesizes the relationship between constitutional provisions and judicial enforcement
activities_projects.evaluation: Technical accuracy in using legal terminology and constitutional logic (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Dalit Narrative Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Understand the psychological and social impact of 'Joothan'
materials: Interview of a contemporary Dalit activist
steps: Read the account of Omprakash being forced to sweep the school while his peers studied
Analyze how such experiences damage a child’s 'Dignity' and 'Self-respect'
Compare Valmiki’s struggle in the 1950s with modern reports of caste-based discrimination in schools
Discuss the concept of 'Institutionalized Inequality'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Critically assesses the long-term impact of caste-based discrimination on human development
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of sociological analysis and empathy in reflection (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Policy Impact: Midday Meal Efficiency
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate the scheme using a SWOT analysis
materials: Government reports on Midday Meal Scheme
steps: Conduct a SWOT (Strengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities, Threats) analysis of the Midday Meal Scheme
Analyze the 'Social Integration' aspect—how it tackles caste and gender bias simultaneously
Identify the logistical failures (corruption, food quality) often reported in news
Propose a 'Digital Monitoring System' to ensure equality in food distribution
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies management tools to evaluate the effectiveness of social welfare policies
activities_projects.evaluation: Comprehensive nature of the SWOT analysis and innovation in proposals (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Comparative Civil Rights Movements
activities_projects.objective: Examine the intersectionality of race and caste
materials: History of the African American Civil Rights Movement
steps: Compare the Civil Rights Act of 1964 (USA) with the SC/ST Prevention of Atrocities Act (India)
Analyze how Rosa Parks' defiance challenged the 'Jim Crow' laws
Discuss the term 'Apartheid' and find parallels in the Indian social structure
Present a comparative essay on 'Resistance against Systemic Discrimination'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Internalizes the global nature of the struggle for equality and human rights
activities_projects.evaluation: Historical accuracy and quality of the comparative analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The 'Mindset' Challenge Project
activities_projects.objective: Address the conflict between legal equality and social attitude
steps: The chapter states: 'Persons are treated unequally even though the law says they are equal'. Identify a specific area (e.g., gender roles or domestic help treatment)
Conduct an anonymous survey on 'Implicit Bias' in your neighborhood
Analyze why democratic laws fail to change 'customary' behaviors quickly
Design a behavioral change campaign using the 'Nudge Theory'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Examines the psychological barriers to social justice in a democratic society
activities_projects.evaluation: Survey methodology and depth of psychological insight (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Health Equity Audit
activities_projects.objective: Investigate the commercialization of basic rights
materials: Cost comparison charts of Public vs. Private hospitals
steps: Analyze the story of Kanta’s daughter—why she could not be taken to the hospital immediately
Evaluate how 'Private Healthcare' creates a class-based hierarchy in the right to health
Research the 'Out-of-Pocket Expenditure' (OOPE) for health in India
Debate: 'Should essential services like health and education be strictly public to ensure equality?'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects economic status to the ability to exercise the 'Right to Life'
activities_projects.evaluation: Strength of debate points and use of health statistics (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: B.R. Ambedkar & The Philosophy of Self-Respect
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the ethics of dignity in political theory
steps: Critically examine Ambedkar’s view that self-respect is the most vital factor in life
Discuss the ethical dilemma: 'Security vs. Self-respect' in the context of the Ansaris
Explain how 'Untouchability' was not just physical but a 'Mental State' of the oppressor
Write a philosophical commentary on 'Dignity as a Non-Negotiable Human Right'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Synthesizes the moral and ethical dimensions of political equality
activities_projects.evaluation: Philosophical depth and clarity of expression (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Media Literacy: Equality vs. Tokenism
activities_projects.objective: Analyze how equality is 'marketed' in contemporary media
steps: Select a 'Social Awareness' advertisement (e.g., on girl child or caste)
Critique whether it addresses the root cause of inequality or is just 'Tokenism'
Observe news debates for 3 days and record how much time is given to 'Grassroots Issues' (rural poverty) vs. 'Urban Elite Issues'
Present a 'Media Critique Report'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates the role of media in shaping or distorting the public's perception of equality
activities_projects.evaluation: Critical analysis of media content and representation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Tawa Matsya Sangh (TMS) Case Study
activities_projects.objective: Examine how collective struggle leads to equality
materials: TMS case study from the latter part of the book/online sources
steps: Study how the displaced people of the Tawa dam fought for their fishing rights
Identify the strategies they used: Rallies, Chakka Jam, and Co-operatives
Explain why 'Collective Action' is often the only way to gain equality for the marginalized
Create a roadmap for a hypothetical movement for 'Right to Equal Playgrounds' in your city
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes 'Struggle' as a vital and legitimate tool in a functioning democracy
activities_projects.evaluation: Analysis of the movement's success and strategic planning of the roadmap (10 marks)
